findings.title: TOCTOU race in port allocation
findings.description: get_free_port() in conftest.py binds to port 0, records the assigned port, closes the socket (releasing it), then returns the port number. There is a window between socket.close() and the caller's bind where another process can claim the same port.
findings.impact: Sporadic test failures due to port collision. Not exploitable for security purposes in a test suite context, but can cause non-deterministic CI failures.
findings.recommendation: Pass SO_REUSEADDR / keep socket open until the proxy binds, or use pytest-randomly with retry logic. Alternatively use a sequential port counter with a lock. Standard workaround: accept the race and mark flaky tests with @pytest.mark.flaky(reruns=2).

findings.title: External CONNECT to example.com:443 without network-availability guard
findings.description: test_connect_tunnel_established (line 164) and test_tls_works_after_restart (lines 308, 324) send CONNECT example.com:443 through the local proxy. The proxy attempts an outbound TCP connection to example.com:443, even though the TLS handshake is never completed. Both tests lack a pytest.skip() guard if the external host is unreachable. The task spec (Details section) explicitly requires: 'pytest.skip() on network-dependent tests when external host is unreachable'.
findings.impact: Tests fail or hang in air-gapped CI environments. Triggers unexpected outbound traffic that may violate network security policies. Spec requirement not met.
findings.recommendation:
Wrap the CONNECT assertion in a try/except and call pytest.skip() on OSError / socket.timeout:

  try:
      sock.sendall(connect_req)
      resp = sock.recv(4096).decode('utf-8', errors='replace')
      assert '200' in resp
  except (OSError, socket.timeout):
      pytest.skip('example.com unreachable')

Or, for full network independence, point CONNECT at the local mock server instead of example.com (mock server does not need to speak TLS for this test — the proxy only checks that the TCP tunnel opens).

findings.title: Broad exception suppression in proxy thread and shutdown code
findings.description: The proxy run() thread suppresses all exceptions with 'except (Exception, SystemExit): pass' (line 75). _shutdown_proxy() similarly silences all errors in two separate bare excepts (lines 94, 98). While acceptable for test teardown, silencing SystemExit from the proxy swallows mitmproxy startup errors that would otherwise surface as meaningful test failures.
findings.impact: Real proxy startup failures (e.g. port permission denied, TLS misconfiguration) are silently swallowed; the test then fails with a generic 'port never opened' message that is harder to diagnose. No security vulnerability, but masks root causes.
